dhp44:2: & this realm of death with all its gods?
dhp44:3: Who will ferret out the well-taught Dhamma-saying,
dhp44:4: as the skillful flower-arranger the flower?
dhp45:1: The learner-on-the-path will penetrate this earth
dhp45:2: & this realm of death with all its gods.
dhp45:3: The learner-on-the-path will ferret out the well-taught Dhamma-saying,
dhp45:4: as the skillful flower-arranger the flower.
dhp46:1: Knowing this body is like foam,
dhp46:3: cutting out the blossoms of Mara,
dhp46:4: you go where the King of Death can’t see.
dhp47:1: The man immersed in gathering blossoms,
dhp47:2:
his heart distracted:
death sweeps him away–
dhp47:3: as a great flood, a village asleep.
dhp48:1: The man immersed in gathering blossoms,
dhp48:4: the End-Maker holds him under his sway.
dhp49:1: As a bee–without harming the blossom,
dhp49:4: so should the sage go through a village.
dhp50:1: Focus, not on the rudenesses of others,
dhp50:2: not on what they’ve done or left undone,
dhp51:4: is fruitless when not carried out.
dhp52:4: is fruitful when well carried out.
dhp53:1: Just as from a heap of flowers
dhp53:2: many garland strands can be made,
dhp53:4: should do –with what’s born & is mortal– many a skillful thing.
dhp54:1: No flower’s scent goes against the wind–
dhp54:3: But the scent of the good does go against the wind.
dhp54:4: The person of integrity wafts a scent in every direction.
dhp55:4: the scent of virtue is unsurpassed.
dhp56:3: while the scent of virtuous conduct
dhp58:1: As in a pile of rubbish
dhp58:2: cast by the side of a highway
dhp58:4:
clean-smelling pleasing the heart, so in the midst of the rubbish-like, people run-of-the-mill & blind,
there dazzles with discernment the disciple of the Rightly Self-Awakened One.
